definition.name: NPA_hierarchy
definition.definition: (Navascués-Pironio-Acín hierarchy). The NPA hierarchy is a sequence of semidefinite programming relaxations that converges to the set of quantum correlations. At level k, one optimizes over (truncated) moment matrices Γ indexed by words of length ≤ k in noncommuting operator variables, subject to positivity Γ ≽ 0 and linear constraints encoding the measurement structure. At convergence, feasible points correspond to states on a universal *-algebra subject to polynomial relations. The Barvinok-Pataki theorem for order unit spaces implies that at any finite level, extremal solutions (which SDP solvers generically find) have GNS representations with bounded commutant dimension Σ n_j² ≤ m+1, providing structural information about optimal quantum strategies. Related: the state polynomial framework of Klep-Magron-Volčič generalises this to noncommutative polynomial optimization. References: Navascués et al., New J. Phys. 10, 073013, 2008; Klep-Magron-Volčič, Math. Program., 2023.
